Intel's semiconductor business shows resilience despite global supply chain challenges, with new chip architectures driving growth. Akzo Nobel, a Dutch chemicals company, navigates energy transition risks while expanding its sustainable products portfolio. Analysts note that tech stocks like Intel are benefiting from AI-driven demand, while industrial firms like Akzo Nobel are adapting to regulatory changes in Europe. Market observers emphasize the importance of tracking these companies' quarterly earnings reports, which will provide insights into sector performance. The article also mentions broader market trends, including inflationary pressures and interest rate expectations, which are influencing investor sen

item.title: Don Iwerks, Oscar-Winning Special Effects Innovator Who Helped Transform Disney Attractions and Filmmaking, Dies at 96
item.description: Don Iwerks, the Academy Award-winning special effects engineer and co-founder of Iwerks Entertainment, died peacefully at the age of 96, according to The Walt Disney Company. The son of legendary Disney animator and inventor Ub Iwerks, Don built upon his father's legacy by helping develop groundbreaking technologies that reshaped both filmmaking and theme park attractions. After serving as a U.S. Army photographer during the Korean War, he joined Disney in 1952, where an unexpected transfer to the Studio Machine Shop launched a decades-long career in engineering and special effects. Throughout his 34 years at Disney, Iwerks contributed to innovations including the Circle-Vision 360 camera system, the endless-loop film projection system, and improvements to photographic techniques used in productions such as 'Mary Poppins.' He also worked on early Disneyland attractions, including 'A Tour of the West,' and even provided the hand model for Abraham Lincoln's Audio-Animatronics figure in '

item.title: Maggie Haberman Reveals Trump's Surprise at High Tariffs on China
item.description: The article discusses how Maggie Haberman, a New York Times correspondent, revealed during an episode of the podcast 'Talk Easy with Sam Fragoso' that President Donald Trump was unaware of the extent of his own tariffs on Chinese goods. According to Haberman's account in her co-authored book 'Regime Change' with Jonathan Swan, Trump did not realize his tariffs had reached 145%, prompting a reaction of disbelief. The revelation highlights a disconnect between Trump's policies and his awareness of their impact. Haberman described the process as driven by instinct rather than analysis, with advisors providing conflicting figures. Trump, who has criticized the book as fiction, is portrayed as eager to reach a deal with China despite escalating tariffs. The article also mentions that Trump paused levies on most countries but kept China's at 145% after market concerns. This incident underscores the chaotic nature of Trump's trade policies and his reliance on gut feelings over strategic plann

item.title: Rare Meteorite Reveals Chemical Fingerprint of Lost Planet from Early Solar System
item.description: A one-pound meteorite discovered in the Sahara in 2019, classified as Northwest Africa 12774 (NWA 12774), has provided evidence for a previously unknown planetary body from the early solar system. This rare angrite meteorite, part of a class of volcanic rocks among the oldest known, contains minerals indicating pressures too high for a small asteroid, suggesting its origin from a Moon-sized or larger planetary embryo. Researchers argue that the mineral composition and pressure readings imply the parent body was large enough to generate intense internal pressures near its surface, rather than deep within a smaller object. The study challenges previous assumptions about meteorite origins, as most are fragments of asteroids rather than planets. NWA 12774's unique chemical fingerprint and volcanic history suggest it may represent a lost world that formed early in the solar system but was destroyed during chaotic planetary collisions. This discovery highlights how meteorites can preserve ev

item.title: Stock Market Volatility Expected as Investors Navigate Uncertain Markets
item.description: The stock market is poised for a volatile trading session as investors grapple with geopolitical tensions, inflationary pressures, and mixed economic data. Major indices like the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq are expected to fluctuate amid concerns over interest rate policies and corporate earnings reports. Analysts warn that market participants should remain cautious due to the fragile economic environment, with potential for sharp swings in both directions. Central bank decisions, particularly from the Federal Reserve, will remain a key focus for traders. Meanwhile, tech stocks may face pressure from rising bond yields, while defensive sectors like utilities and healthcare could see increased demand. Market observers are closely monitoring economic indicators such as the nonfarm payrolls report and consumer price index data for clues about the Federal Reserve's stance on interest rates. Investors are advised to maintain diversified portfolios and avoid overexposur

item.title: Bluesky's Toni Schneider Becomes Permanent CEO After Four Months as Interim Leader
item.description: Toni Schneider, the founding CEO of Automattic (parent company of WordPress and Tumblr), has transitioned from interim CEO to permanent chief executive of Bluesky. Schneider, who took over after Jay Graber's departure in March 2026, announced the change in a blog post, emphasizing his commitment to the company's mission. His priorities include creating smaller, more private communities to drive growth and innovation. Bluesky, originally spun off from Twitter, faced challenges in retaining users amid declining engagement and a post-Trump re-election user drop. Under Graber, the platform reached 43 million users, but recent struggles have raised concerns about its viability. Schneider's role as a partner at True Ventures and his investment in Bluesky add to his stake in the company's future. The AT Protocol, Bluesky's underlying technology, has seen expansion but requires renewed focus to address user retention issues. Schneider's transition marks a pivotal moment for Bluesky as it navig

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel mocks Mitch McConnell's proof-of-life photo after weeks of speculation
item.description: Late-night host Jimmy Kimmel returned from summer vacation to mock Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell's long-awaited proof-of-life photo, which was released nearly a month after the 84-year-old Kentucky Republican was hospitalized following a medical emergency. The image showed McConnell sitting up beside his wife, former Trump Transportation Secretary Elaine Chao, holding a copy of Sunday's Washington Post. McConnell's office claimed he briefly lost consciousness after a fall and had mild pneumonia but insisted he did not suffer a stroke, heart attack, concussion, or broken bones. The photo sparked skepticism amid weeks of online speculation about McConnell's health, especially following news of fellow Senator Lindsey Graham's death from a sudden illness. Kimmel responded with a Photoshop image featuring his head on McConnell's body, captioning it, 'For those who've been asking, I'm feeling great.' The post drew reactions from celebrities and politicians, including actor Henry Win

item.title: ICE Faces Scrutiny Over Lack of Evidence in Houston Shooting Incident
item.description: The article examines the controversy surrounding the shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents, highlighting the absence of conclusive evidence and conflicting narratives. It details how ICE's initial claims about the incident have been met with skepticism, drawing parallels to past high-profile cases where similar statements were later discredited. The text emphasizes the lack of video footage, surveillance data, or ballistic analysis to corroborate ICE's account, raising questions about transparency and accountability. Local officials in Houston have criticized federal investigators for withholding access to critical evidence, including the van, passengers, and deceased individual involved. Law enforcement experts stress the importance of cooperation between federal and local authorities to maintain public trust, noting that the current situation risks appearing as a cover-up. The piece also underscores broader concerns about the use

item.title: New Zealand Actor Sam Neill, 'Jurassic Park' Star, Dies at 78
item.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, renowned for his role as paleontologist Dr. Alan Grant in the 'Jurassic Park' film series, has passed away at the age of 78. His family announced his death through a social media post, emphasizing that he died with dignity surrounded by loved ones. Neill, who began his acting career in the late 1960s, was celebrated for his distinctive voice and striking appearance. His extensive filmography includes critically acclaimed works such as 'The Piano' and 'Hunt for the Wilderpeople,' as well as popular franchises like 'Thor: Love and Thunder.' He also starred in the British drama series 'Peaky Blinders.' Neill's battle with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, diagnosed in early 2022, was a significant chapter in his life. Australian political leaders, including Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, paid tribute to his cultural impact, noting his resilience and artistry. His legacy endures through his iconic performances and contributions to global cinema.

item.title: Sen. Rick Scott Calls for Senate Reform to Address Productivity and Legislative Delays
item.description: Sen. Rick Scott criticizes the U.S. Senate's declining productivity, arguing that lawmakers should emulate the work ethic of American workers who dedicate five days a week to their jobs. He highlights the Senate's current inefficiency, noting that the chamber has only been in session for 79 days this year and has failed to pass critical legislation such as the SAVE America Act, which aims to secure elections. Scott emphasizes that the Senate's frequent adjournments—often leaving work unfinished by Thursday afternoon—hinder progress on vital issues like government funding, spending cuts, and election security. He calls for a return to a more rigorous legislative schedule, urging Senators to stay in Washington until tasks are completed rather than prioritizing personal time over public responsibility. Scott attributes the Senate's sluggishness to decades of bipartisan decline, stressing that this dysfunction undermines American interests and the trust of constituents. His comments come a

item.title: Opinion columnist argues Trump's social media post combined longstanding attacks on Obama, race, and Islam
item.description: This opinion article argues that a recent Truth Social post by President Donald Trump reflects what the author describes as three recurring themes in Trump's public messaging: criticism of former President Barack Obama, fixation on Air Force One, and rhetoric involving race and Islam. The article focuses on an AI-generated image that Trump shared depicting Barack and Michelle Obama boarding the previous Air Force One, with the aircraft altered to display graffiti including 'BLM,' 'Yes We Can,' and an Arabic phrase. According to the columnist, these additions were intended to portray the Obamas as foreign, disorderly, or illegitimate through racial and cultural stereotypes. The author also connects the post to previous incidents, including other altered images targeting the Obamas, arguing that they form part of a broader pattern. A central argument is that Trump's use of Arabic language and Muslim imagery is hypocritical because he recently accepted and celebrated the use of a Boeing 7

item.title: Arsenal and Atletico Madrid in transfer tug-of-war over Julian Alvarez
item.description: Arsenal has intensified its pursuit of Atletico Madrid forward Julian Alvarez, aiming to finalize the transfer before pre-season begins. The Gunners are capitalizing on Atletico's refusal to sell to Barcelona, which has strained their relationship with the Spanish club. Alvarez's recent heroics in Argentina's World Cup quarter-final victory against Switzerland have boosted his market value, making him a top target for Arsenal. However, Atletico remains firm on their stance, rejecting offers from Barcelona and Real Madrid due to perceived public campaigns targeting the player. The valuation gap between Arsenal's £90 million offer and Atletico's demand of over £100 million poses a challenge. Arsenal sporting director Andrea Berta, formerly of Atletico, is negotiating with his former employers, leveraging existing relationships to bridge the price difference. Alvarez's Premier League experience with Manchester City, where he won two titles and a Champions League trophy, adds value to his

item.title: New Zealand Actor Sam Neill Dies at 78 After Cancer Recovery
item.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill passed away suddenly at the age of 78 on Monday, July 13, 2026, following his recovery from stage-three blood cancer. His family announced his death in a statement shared on his Instagram page, describing it as 'sudden and unexpected' but noting that he had remained cancer-free for some time. Neill, best known for his role as Dr. Alan Grant in the Jurassic Park franchise, was surrounded by loved ones at the time of his passing. His family expressed gratitude for his quality of life during treatment and emphasized their request for privacy as they grieve. Beyond Jurassic Park, Neill appeared in films such as The Piano, Event Horizon, and The Portable Door. His journey from cancer diagnosis to remission garnered public attention, with fans celebrating his resilience. The news highlights both his personal battle with illness and his legacy in cinema.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates with Strikes and Diplomatic Tensions
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between the United States and Iran, marked by a series of military strikes and diplomatic clashes. U.S. President Donald Trump authorized strikes against Iranian military targets to disrupt Iran's ability to attack civilian mariners and commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran retaliated by launching drone and missile attacks on U.S. military bases in the region, including Bahrain and Kuwait. The conflict has led to significant global repercussions, particularly in oil markets, where Brent crude and U.S. crude prices rose by over 3% due to heightened tensions. The dispute over the Strait of Hormuz's status remains a critical point, with the U.S. insisting the waterway is open while Iran claims it is closed after warning shots fired at unauthorized vessels. Diplomatic efforts, including Oman's proposed traffic management plan, have failed to de-escalate the situation. The article also highlights the impact on global energy markets and th

item.title: Citizen Science Reveals Evolutionary Pathways of Parental Care in Harvestmen
item.description: A recent study highlights how citizen scientists, using platforms like iNaturalist, have contributed to understanding the evolutionary development of parental care in harvestmen (small arachnids). Researchers analyzed data from thousands of observations, doubling the number of documented cases of maternal and paternal behaviors in these creatures. The study reveals that maternal care evolved first, with females guarding eggs, while paternal care emerged later through distinct evolutionary pathways. This discovery challenges previous assumptions about the universality of certain caregiving behaviors. The research underscores the critical role of community science in biodiversity studies, enabling large-scale data collection that would be impossible for traditional methods alone. Scientists emphasize that while taxonomists are essential for classification, the public's participation has revolutionized ecological research. The findings also suggest that evolutionary processes in invertebr

item.title: Gum Disease Bacteria Linked to Heart Valve Damage Risk
item.description: A new study suggests that bacteria associated with gum disease, specifically Porphyromonas gingivalis, may contribute to calcific aortic valve stenosis (CAVS) by triggering inflammation and calcium buildup in heart valves. Researchers analyzed bacterial levels in heart valve tissue from patients undergoing surgery and found higher presence of P. gingivalis in CAVS cases compared to other valve conditions. Mouse experiments showed that live P. gingivalis exposure led to valve calcification and aortic stenosis symptoms, while antibiotics and genetic removal of the IL-1β pathway reduced these effects. The findings highlight a potential biological mechanism connecting periodontal disease to cardiovascular issues, though the results are preliminary and require human confirmation. The American Heart Association emphasizes the importance of oral health in overall cardiovascular wellness, noting that dental professionals could play a key role in early detection of periodontal disease. While no

item.title: Paula Prentiss: A 1960s Comedy Icon's Legacy
item.description: Paula Prentiss, an American actress renowned for her sharp wit, radiant smile, and comedic talent during the 1960s, emerged as a prominent figure in Hollywood. Born Paula Ragusa in 1938 in San Antonio, Texas, she gained fame for her fresh charm and tall, striking presence. Her career spanned notable films such as *Where the Boys Are* (1960), *Bachelor in Paradise* (1961), and the iconic *What’s New Pussycat?* (1965), where she co-starred with Woody Allen and Peter O’Toole. Often paired with her real-life husband, actor Richard Benjamin, Prentiss delivered lively and intelligent performances that made her one of the most likable stars of her generation. Though she stepped away from the spotlight in the 1970s to focus on family, Prentiss remains a beloved figure in classic Hollywood comedy. These vintage photos capture her radiant smile, sparkling personality, and natural elegance, highlighting her enduring impact on 1960s cinema.

item.title: Major Storm Causes Power Outages in North Central Saskatchewan
item.description: A major storm struck north central Saskatchewan on July 11, 2026, causing widespread power outages that left the city of Prince Albert in darkness around 10:50 p.m. The storm brought destructive winds, heavy rain, hail, and lightning, leading to localized flooding, downed trees, and significant damage. SaskPower reported restoring power to most areas, including Prince Albert City, Shellbrook, and surrounding rural regions, by 6:30 a.m. Sunday. However, crews continued working to restore electricity to Tisdale, Nipawin, and other affected areas. The storm also prompted reports of tornadoes, with at least 10 confirmed in the province. Emergency crews emphasized caution, advising residents to avoid driving and to check for downed power lines. The incident highlights the vulnerability of infrastructure to extreme weather events and the importance of preparedness in regions prone to such storms.

item.title: California legislators push to exempt film tax credit program from corporate tax cap
item.description: Over 30 California legislators are urging Governor Gavin Newsom to exempt the state's film and TV production incentive program from a newly imposed cap on corporate tax credits. The letter, addressed to Newsom and key legislative leaders, warns that without exemption, the program will be 'significantly kneecapped,' jeopardizing thousands of middle-class jobs in the entertainment industry. The budget law limits annual tax credits to $5 million or 50% of a company's state tax liability, which could undermine the $750 million annual cap established last year. Industry representatives argue the change will reverse progress made in attracting production back to California after pandemic-related shutdowns, strikes, and industry exodus. Assemblyman Rick Chavez Zbur, who co-authored the 2025 bill expanding eligibility, called the cap a 'complete decimation' for the sector. The film commission projects $6.6 billion in direct spending and over 34,000 jobs from the incentive program, highlighting

item.title: Survivor of the Gardelegen Massacre Shares His Escape Story with U.S. Forces
item.description: The article recounts the harrowing events of the Gardelegen massacre in April 1945 during World War II. On April 13, SS troops and local auxiliaries herded over 1,000 slave laborers and concentration camp prisoners into a barn near Gardelegen. The captives were forced to endure a brutal death march from camps like Mittelbau-Dora and Hannover-Stöcken before being lined up inside the barn. Guards doused the floor with gasoline-soaked straw and used phosphorus grenades to ignite the structure, killing most of the prisoners. Those who tried to escape through doors or walls were machine-gunned by guards outside. The U.S. Army arrived less than 24 hours later, discovering the perpetrators in the act of burning bodies to cover up evidence. Only 11-20 survivors, including Bonde Gaza, were found alive. The military then ordered local citizens to exhume remains and give victims proper burials. The article highlights the atrocities committed by Nazi forces and the resilience of survivors like Gaz

item.title: Exploring Innovations in Robotics, Nature, and Design: July 5–12, 2026
item.description: The article highlights a variety of topics spanning technology, nature, and design. It begins with a discussion about Weave Robotics' Isaac, an $8,000 laundry folding robot, raising questions about trust in home automation. Next, it introduces the Serow, a unique goat-antelope species found in Japan, emphasizing rare wildlife discoveries. The piece also explores Fordite, an accidental gemstone formed from decades of car paint overspray in Detroit, now extinct as no new batches will be made. Environmental concerns are addressed through a segment on hammerhead sharks, whose populations have declined by 97% since the 1990s, with a National Geographic film advocating for their protection. Related content includes stories about Australia's firehawks—birds that intentionally spread wildfires to hunt prey—and the Morning Glory cloud, a 1,000 km-long cloud surfed by glider pilots. The article also touches on architectural concepts, such as a hypothetical Antoni Gaudí-designed hotel in New York

item.title: Meta Removes AI Feature on Instagram Following User Backlash
item.description: Meta has removed a controversial AI feature from Instagram after facing significant backlash from users and stakeholders. The feature, which allowed users to modify photos from public Instagram accounts using AI, was part of a batch of new AI tools rolled out earlier this week. The company's AI unit, Meta Superintelligence Labs, introduced Muse Image, an AI image generator, and a feature enabling users to generate images by @-mentioning public accounts. However, this feature sparked immediate criticism due to privacy concerns and potential misuse, such as generating explicit content without consent. TechCrunch highlighted the issue and provided guidance on disabling the feature. Meta later issued a blog post acknowledging the feedback, stating the feature 'missed the mark' and was no longer available. The decision comes amid scrutiny from users, talent agencies, and regulatory bodies, reflecting broader challenges in balancing AI innovation with ethical and privacy considerations. The

item.title: Deep-Sea Microbes Benefit from Pressure-Induced Nutrient Release
item.description: Scientists from the University of Southern Denmark discovered that extreme deep-sea pressure forces nutrients out of sinking organic particles, creating an unexpected food source for ocean microbes. This process, observed in marine snow (tiny clumps of dead algae and organic material), releases up to 50% of carbon and 58-63% of nitrogen as the particles descend 2-6 kilometers. The findings challenge previous assumptions about deep-ocean ecosystems and the carbon cycle, suggesting that significant carbon may remain suspended in deep waters rather than being buried in sediments. The study, published in Science Advances, highlights how hydrostatic pressure acts like a 'juicer,' squeezing dissolved organic matter accessible to microbes. This discovery could reshape understanding of carbon storage and its role in climate processes. Researchers plan to validate these findings in the Arctic Ocean, where they hope to detect molecular signatures of this pressure-driven leakage. The implications

item.title: Rare Color Photographs Reveal 1939 Golden Gate International Exposition's Art Deco Splendor
item.description: The article highlights a collection of rare color slides capturing the 1939 Golden Gate International Exposition, a pivotal world's fair held on Treasure Island in San Francisco Bay. Celebrating the completion of the Golden Gate Bridge and the San Francisco–Oakland Bay Bridge, the exposition showcased Art Deco architecture, towering structures, lush gardens, and futuristic pavilions. These early color photographs provide a vivid glimpse into the event's festive atmosphere, elegant 1930s fashion, and the optimistic spirit of pre-war America. The slides preserve a unique moment in history, offering insights into the cultural and architectural achievements of the era. The article emphasizes the significance of these images as a visual treasure, highlighting their rarity and the historical context of the exposition's grandeur. The exposition's legacy endures through these photographs, which document a blend of innovation, artistry, and the aspirations of a nation on the cusp of major globa

item.title: PG&E Settles $22M Fine for Mosquito Fire Violations
item.description: Regulators in California are seeking $22 million from PG&E, alleging the utility provider committed violations linked to the 2022 Mosquito fire in Placer County. The fire, which ignited near the Oxbow Reservoir on September 6, 2022, burned over 76,788 acres and destroyed 78 structures. The California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) found evidence that PG&E failed to repair or maintain certain power lines, though these failures did not directly cause the fire. The utility was criticized for delaying fire notifications and destroying critical equipment before investigators could examine it. PG&E's director of risk and electric compliance, Daniel Kushner, stated the pole and equipment were mistakenly destroyed due to internal miscoordination. The proposed settlement includes a $21 million penalty to the state’s general fund and up to $1 million for a third-party review of PG&E’s policies. The CPUC will vote on the proposal on August 13. The case highlights ongoing scrutiny of utility c

item.title: Cheryl Ladd's Role in the 1986 ABC Miniseries 'Crossings'
item.description: This article highlights Cheryl Ladd's portrayal of Liane DeVilliers in the 1986 ABC television miniseries 'Crossings,' an adaptation of Danielle Steel's bestselling World War II romance novel. The piece details her glamorous costuming, including era-specific 1930s-and-1940s updos and outfits that reflect her character's transformation from a wealthy ambassador's wife to a wartime nurse. For pre-war scenes on a transatlantic ocean liner, Ladd wore elegant evening gowns, silk gloves, and diamond necklaces, while her wardrobe shifted to structured day suits and a Red Cross nursing uniform as the plot progressed. The production, directed by Karen Arthur and produced by Aaron Spelling Productions, required meticulous period-accurate set designs and location shooting to recreate WWII-era settings. Ladd's on-set photos with co-stars Christopher Plummer and Lee Horsley showcase the collaborative effort behind the massive production. The article emphasizes the historical context and fashion det

item.title: How Tone and Cultural References Influence Curiosity in Science Content
item.description: A study by the University of Georgia explored how different factors affect viewer curiosity when engaging with science content on social media. Researchers analyzed over 1,000 participants' reactions to TikTok and Instagram-style videos about jellyfish, finding that casual tone and pop culture references like SpongeBob SquarePants increased enjoyment and curiosity. However, cultural and racial differences influenced responses: Black audiences preferred non-cartoonish content, while race-congruent presenters enhanced curiosity for specific demographics. The study emphasizes balancing curiosity-sparking elements with partial answers to sustain interest. Key findings include the importance of tailoring content to audience sensibilities, avoiding assumptions about cultural understanding, and creating concise, engaging videos that encourage further exploration. Published in *Science Communication*, the research highlights the need for science creators to adapt their messaging to diverse gro

item.title: U.S. Citizen in Congo Tests Positive for Ebola as Outbreak Escalates
item.description: A U.S. citizen working for a humanitarian organization in the Democratic Republic of Congo has tested positive for the Ebola virus, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). The CDC is collaborating with the individual's employer, U.S. agencies, Congolese health authorities, and international partners to prevent further transmission and trace close contacts. The State Department confirmed awareness of the case and is supporting the affected American citizen. The outbreak, driven by the rare Bundibugyo virus, has become the fastest-growing Ebola epidemic in Africa, with over 1,830 confirmed cases and 648 deaths in Congo, including spread to Uganda. The CDC highlighted the challenges posed by the outbreak, including the lack of approved vaccines or treatments, ongoing conflict in eastern Congo displacing populations, and mistrust in health centers. Healthcare workers face inadequate protective gear, exacerbating risks. The situation underscores the need for globa

item.title: Political commentators criticize Supreme Court's handling of Trump's birthright citizenship case
item.description: The article reports on reactions from political commentator David Pakman and independent journalist Katie Phang following a recent Supreme Court decision involving President Donald Trump and birthright citizenship. According to the report, although the Court formally decided the case known as Trump v. Barbara by a 6-3 vote, four justices indicated they might be open to arguments that could allow the executive branch to reinterpret the meaning of the 14th Amendment regarding birthright citizenship. The article argues that such a position would represent a significant departure from the constitutional amendment process, which requires congressional approval and ratification by the states rather than unilateral executive action. Trump has reportedly requested that the Supreme Court rehear the case, an uncommon step that, if granted, would make it one of the few cases the Court has reconsidered after issuing a decision. During an episode of The David Pakman Show, Pakman described the justi

item.title: U.S. and Iran Clash Over Strait of Hormuz Status Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran are locked in a bitter dispute over the status of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway through which a significant portion of the world's oil shipments pass. The U.S. military has conducted multiple strikes against Iranian targets in the region, citing Iran's alleged attacks on commercial vessels. Meanwhile, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has claimed to have closed the strait, asserting that it is no longer safe for ships to transit. However, U.S. Central Command and the Joint Maritime Information Center insist the strait remains open, emphasizing their commitment to ensuring freedom of navigation. The conflict has raised concerns about the potential disruption of global energy supplies and the broader implications for international trade. Analysts warn that the situation could escalate further, with both sides accusing each other of provocative actions. The U.S. has also warned of the economic consequences of any disruption to the strai

item.title: AtkinsRéalis Extends Civil Works Role at Sizewell C Nuclear Plant
item.description: AtkinsRéalis, a construction and engineering firm formerly known as SNC Lavalin, has signed a five-year framework agreement to continue its civil works role at the Sizewell C nuclear power plant in the UK. The agreement positions AtkinsRéalis as the design partner for the permanent civil works of the project, encompassing multidisciplinary design and engineering services across the plant's infrastructure. This includes the conventional island, balance of plant systems, heat sink buildings, and ancillary works. The company has been involved in the Sizewell C project since 2019, contributing to design and engineering management. The Sizewell C project is a significant endeavor in the UK's nuclear energy strategy, aiming to provide low-carbon electricity to millions of households. The agreement underscores the ongoing collaboration between private engineering firms and government-backed nuclear initiatives. The civil works phase is critical for ensuring the structural integrity and operat

item.title: Phia faces allegations of affiliate fraud over alleged cookie stuffing practices
item.description: Phia, a shopping startup co-founded by Phoebe Gates and Sophia Kianni, is under investigation for alleged 'cookie stuffing' practices that may have allowed the company to claim commissions for sales it did not generate. A Bloomberg report alleges that Phia injected its referral codes into users' shopping sessions, overriding other affiliates' codes to take credit for purchases made through its platform. This practice, which has led to Phia's suspension from Impact.com, mirrors tactics previously used by Honey, which faces an ongoing class-action lawsuit. The startup, which raised over $40 million in funding and has celebrity investors like Khloé Kardashian and Hailey Bieber, operates an app that helps users find discounted items across retailers. While Phia claims fixes were implemented, retailers and partners remain skeptical. The controversy highlights broader issues in affiliate marketing, where competing platforms often engage in similar practices to maximize commissions. The case

item.title: Analyst Reflects on Lindsey Graham's Death and His Motivations for Supporting Trump
item.description: The article discusses the death of Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) and an analysis of his decision to support President Donald Trump. Political analyst Will Saletan, writing for The Bulwark, suggests Graham's actions were driven by a desire to protect the 'soul' of the Republican Party and gain influence over Trump's foreign policy. Saletan notes Graham's efforts to 'bring back' Trump after the 2020 election, despite the president's loss, and highlights the irony that Graham's death prevents him from seeing the potential consequences of his actions. Analysts argue Graham's death is a significant blow to the Trump administration's efforts to mend relations with Congress. The piece also touches on other political developments, including Mitch McConnell's health disclosure and Democratic momentum in key races, but the central focus remains on Graham's legacy and the broader implications of his support for Trump.

item.title: Democrats Gain Momentum in Maine Senate Race After Candidate Drops Out
item.description: The article discusses how Democrats have gained significant momentum in the Maine Senate race following the withdrawal of candidate Graham Platner. Platner suspended his campaign after multiple allegations of sexual harassment and rape were raised against him, which he denied as false. Despite initial predictions that the race would be a toss-up, prediction markets now show Democrats with a 63% chance of winning. Analyst Harry Enten noted that Platner's underperformance relative to Maine's political fundamentals has shifted the advantage toward Democrats. Enten highlighted Susan Collins' long history of winning Senate elections in Maine but warned that her current challenges are unprecedented. With Platner out of the race, Collins faces an uphill battle to secure re-election, as a generic Democratic candidate now holds a 12-point lead over her. The article underscores the impact of candidate withdrawals on electoral dynamics and the shifting landscape of Senate races ahead of the 2026

item.title: Mitch McConnell's Health Statement Sparks Analysis on Political Transparency
item.description: U.S. Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-KY) faced scrutiny after his recent hospitalization and a public statement about his health, which political analyst Robert Davis described as a 'defining moment' in the saga. The statement, which revealed McConnell's vulnerability due to age and past health issues, raised concerns about transparency and his ability to serve. Analyst Camila DeChalus highlighted the significance of McConnell's openness, noting that his previous hospitalizations were publicly shared, but this instance remained silent. The situation gained attention when Kentucky's Democratic governor requested a health update, emphasizing constituents' right to know about a senator's fitness for office. DeChalus called the moment 'key' for American politics, as it underscored tensions between political figures' personal health and public accountability. The article also touches on broader debates about transparency in leadership, with critics arguing that voters deserve clar

item.title: Lindsey Graham Dies at 71 from Aortic Dissection, Medical Examiner Reports
item.description: The article details the sudden death of U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham, who passed away on July 11, 2026, at the age of 71 from a brief and sudden illness. Emergency dispatch audio reviewed by Fox News Digital captures the cardiac arrest call at his Washington, D.C., home, showing first responders were dispatched shortly after 8 p.m. The audio reveals the front door was initially reported unlocked but later found locked, prompting police assistance for forced entry. CPR was in progress 20-25 minutes after the initial call. The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner for the District of Columbia released preliminary findings indicating Graham died from an aortic dissection caused by arteriosclerotic cardiovascular disease. The death certificate remains pending until toxicology and microscopic testing are complete. Graham, a long-time South Carolina senator and retired U.S. Air Force colonel, had recently met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Kyiv and was scheduled to appear on N

item.title: Lindsey Graham's foreign policy legacy and impact on US global strategy
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, a prominent Republican from South Carolina, passed away at 71, leaving a significant void in American foreign policy. Known for his deep understanding of international affairs, Graham bridged the gap between intelligence reports and public understanding, translating complex geopolitical issues into accessible language. His advocacy for Ukraine, support for Israel, and strategic engagement with Iran's regime shaped U.S. foreign policy. Graham's ability to work with both Democrats and Republicans, rebuild the defense budget, and maintain relationships with leaders like McCain and Trump highlighted his bipartisan approach. Iranian dissidents praised his advocacy, and he consistently prioritized American security over personal gain. His legacy includes strengthening U.S.-Israel alliances, promoting the Abraham Accords, and resisting narrative warfare. Graham's death marks a pivotal moment for American foreign policy, as his strategic insights and moral clarity will be

item.title: North Carolina Senate Race Faces Financial and Strategic Challenges
item.description: The North Carolina Senate race between Democratic incumbent Roy Cooper and Republican nominee Michael Whatley highlights stark disparities in campaign funding and national party support. Cooper, a former governor with broad name recognition, leads Whatley in both polls and fundraising, having raised $13.8 million compared to Whatley's $5 million in the first quarter. Republicans argue that Whatley's lack of name ID and the national GOP's delayed financial support hinder his campaign, especially as the race is overshadowed by more prominent contests in Texas, Maine, and Michigan. The race is critical for Democrats seeking to flip the Senate, with Cooper's experience and national profile giving him an edge. Republicans plan to focus on crime and pandemic policies, criticizing Cooper for pandemic-era decisions like releasing inmates and restricting church activities. Meanwhile, the GOP's financial advantage, bolstered by $125 million in the RNC's treasury, contrasts with the DNC's debt, c

item.title: New Study Reveals How the Brain Rewires for Multitasking
item.description: A recent study published in the Journal of Cognitive Neuroscience sheds light on how the human brain adapts to multitasking by reorganizing neural pathways. Researchers at Georgetown University Medical Center found that the brain employs modular 'cognitive blocks' to manage complex tasks, a concept previously explored by Princeton University in 2025. The study highlights that the prefrontal cortex plays a critical role in parsing and storing memory fragments, while the hippocampus handles overall experiences. This rewiring allows individuals to switch between tasks efficiently, challenging the notion that multitasking is inherently inefficient. The findings have implications for understanding productivity, cognitive science, and even artificial intelligence. The research also addresses long-standing debates about whether the brain operates through unified systems or separate modules for memory and attention. By demonstrating the brain's adaptability, the study offers new insights into

item.title: Rep. Ro Khanna Withdraws Support for Graham Platner Following Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., expressed regret over his past endorsement of Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner following new allegations of sexual assault. Platner suspended his campaign after a report accused him of sexually assaulting an ex-girlfriend in 2021. Khanna admitted he misjudged the situation, acknowledging that previous controversies, including Platner's Nazi tattoo and blaming survivors of sexual assault, should have been red flags. He emphasized that his decision to withdraw support came only after the sexual assault allegation surfaced, despite earlier downplaying the claims by citing Platner's PTSD as a combat veteran. Khanna criticized the Democratic Party's collective failure to address the issue earlier, while also defending Platner's progressive policies on foreign wars and Medicare for All. The incident highlights ongoing debates within the party about accountability for sexual misconduct allegations and the balance between political ideology and persona

item.title: Mitch McConnell Shares Hospitalization Details After Month-Long Stay
item.description: U.S. Senator Mitch McConnell (R-KY) broke his silence about his medical condition after a month-long hospitalization, sharing details of his health struggles in a statement that sparked mixed reactions. The senator, who has been hospitalized since late June, released a photo of himself sitting upright in his hospital bed, dressed in a red plaid shirt, holding a newspaper alongside his wife, former Transportation Secretary Elaine Chao. In the statement, McConnell disclosed that he had fallen and was briefly unconscious, leading to his hospitalization. His doctors confirmed he did not suffer broken bones, a concussion, heart attack, stroke, tumors, or hemorrhages, though he contracted a mild case of pneumonia. Critics, including journalists and political commentators, reacted to the timing of the statement, noting the proximity to the death of Senator Lindsey Graham, who died of a cardiac event hours earlier. Some questioned the lack of transparency surrounding McConnell's condition, whi

item.title: Former South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham Dies After Sudden Illness
item.description: Lindsey Graham, a longtime Republican senator from South Carolina, passed away on Saturday night following a brief illness, according to his office. The news has sparked tributes from President Donald Trump and others who highlighted his three-decade career in Congress and his reputation as a staunch foreign policy advocate. Graham, who was 71, died hours after returning from a trip to Ukraine. Trump expressed personal grief, stating he spoke with Graham on Saturday evening and that the conversation could have been his last. The senator was known for his conservative stance and frequent criticism of the Biden administration's foreign policy. His death marks the end of an era for South Carolina politics, where he was a prominent figure in the Republican Party. Colleagues and political analysts have praised his dedication to national security and his role in shaping U.S. foreign policy during critical moments, including the Iraq War and the War on Terror. Graham's passing has also prompt

item.title: Trump administration subpoenas journalists over Air Force One story, experts call it troubling
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's administration has subpoenaed two New York Times reporters for a story revealing that he used a Qatari-funded Air Force One to travel to the UK instead of the regular Air Force One during a NATO summit in Turkey. Media expert Brian Stelter criticized the move as 'very troubling,' noting it represents an aggressive attempt by the Trump DOJ to compel journalists to testify and reveal anonymous sources. The Times plans to fight the subpoenas, with executive editor Joseph Kahn stating that the law protects journalists from such retaliatory actions. Stelter highlighted that previous attempts by the Trump administration to force journalists to disclose sources have been struck down by courts, but this case is unique due to its rapid timing after the story's publication. The subpoena raises concerns about press freedom and the government's overreach in using national security as a pretext.

item.title: WWE Champion Rhea Ripley discusses meniscus injury and recovery progress
item.description: Rhea Ripley, the WWE Women's Champion, has been sidelined due to a slight meniscus tear in her knee. During a recent appearance at a Power Slap event, Ripley provided an update on her health, explaining that the injury occurred in a 'weird spot' and is affecting her ability to bend or crouch. She mentioned being out for approximately a month and a week, with her knee still experiencing pain and fatigue. While her knee feels better, she notes it remains tender and limits her mobility. Ripley, who recently defended her title against Jade Cargill at WrestleMania 42, has been absent from WWE programming, including 'Friday Night SmackDown,' due to her recovery. Her return timeline remains uncertain, as the injury's healing process depends on her body's response. Despite the setback, Ripley remains optimistic about her recovery and her continued role in WWE. The article highlights her status as one of the most popular female superstars in the company and her ongoing efforts to balance her he

item.title: Texas Hispanics Shift Support to Talarico Amid Immigration Policy Concerns
item.description: A recent survey of Hispanic business owners in Texas reveals a significant shift in political support, with many moving away from Republican candidates due to concerns over Trump's immigration policies. The poll, commissioned by the U.S. Hispanic Business Council, found that one in five respondents reported having an employee deported in the past year, while seven in ten said their businesses were impacted by Trump's tariffs. Despite a plurality of respondents identifying as Republicans, Democratic nominee James Talarico holds a seven-point lead over Attorney General Ken Paxton. The survey highlights growing dissatisfaction among Hispanic voters, who are critical of the Trump administration's immigration enforcement actions and economic policies. Business owners describe the deportation crisis as creating fear and disrupting operations, with small businesses particularly affected. The findings underscore broader challenges for Republicans in Texas, where Hispanic communities have histo

item.title: International University Partnerships Drive Sustainable Development and One Health Initiatives in the Caribbean
item.description: The article highlights collaborative efforts between universities in Grenada, Namibia, and other regions to address global challenges through interdisciplinary approaches. Key initiatives include the One Health program, which integrates human, animal, and environmental health to combat zoonotic diseases like COVID-19. Students engage in community-based projects such as veterinary care for livestock and free health screenings. In Namibia, partnerships focus on geothermal energy and green hydrogen development, aiming to create sustainable job opportunities while assessing local impacts. Higher education institutions are positioned as critical engines for achieving the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 7 (Affordable and Clean Energy) and SDG 13 (Climate Action). The article emphasizes the need for universities to adapt to societal demands by fostering innovation, research, and workforce development, moving away from traditional educational models. It underscores th

item.title: DOJ's Admission of Epstein Files Could Lead to Legal Challenges
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) under former President Donald Trump faces potential legal repercussions after admitting to possessing key files related to the Jeffrey Epstein case. Analysts warn that this admission could backfire dramatically, as the DOJ's denial of a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to obtain training materials for redacting Epstein files has sparked controversy. The DOJ cited privacy concerns, sensitive law enforcement techniques, and attorney-client privilege to withhold the documents, but legal experts argue these justifications may not hold. The case highlights the DOJ's struggle with transparency, as similar exemptions were previously rejected in cases involving former Attorney General Bill Barr and lawyer John Eastman. Critics suggest the DOJ's repeated use of these exemptions, combined with allegations of misconduct surrounding the Epstein files, could lead to a loss of judicial trust. The situation underscores growing scrutiny of the Trump admini

item.title: Medical Examiner Reveals Cause of Death for Late Senator Lindsey Graham
item.description: Late U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died after suffering an aortic dissection caused by arteriosclerotic cardiovascular disease, according to a report from the District of Columbia Medical Examiner's Office. The findings, released by Graham's office on July 12, 2026, indicate that the fatal tear in Graham's aorta was linked to fatty plaques restricting blood flow. While the official death certificate remains pending until toxicological and microscopic tests are completed, the medical examiner's preliminary report provides critical insight into the circumstances of his passing. Graham, a prominent Republican senator known for his close ties to former President Donald Trump, died late Saturday night after being rushed from his Washington, D.C., home to George Washington University Hospital. The report highlights the complex interplay between chronic cardiovascular conditions and acute medical emergencies, underscoring the importance of early detection and management of such diseases.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Regulatory Challenges and Industry Shifts in Autonomous Vehicles
item.description: The article discusses the evolving landscape of autonomous vehicle (AV) technology, focusing on regulatory challenges and industry dynamics. Key points include the impending end of Uber-Waymo partnerships in Phoenix, with ongoing collaborations in Atlanta and Austin. Analysts speculate that tensions between companies will escalate post-partnership, particularly in policy battles over market access. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) administrator Jonathan Morrison issued a directive emphasizing the critical need for AVs to prioritize first responder interactions, citing Waymo's repeated incidents as a focal point. The article also highlights Rivian's $1.32 billion funding round, its sales forecasts, and other industry developments like Bidbus' $15 million raise and TaiSan's £4.65 million seed funding. Additional coverage includes a data breach at AssuranceAmerica, Beta Technologies' eVTOL flights, and Manna Aero's expansion. The piece underscores the regulatory and

item.title: South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham’s Death Creates Senate Vacancy Amid Political Uncertainty
item.description: The sudden death of longtime South Carolina Republican Senator Lindsey Graham has created an unexpected vacancy in the U.S. Senate, complicating President Donald Trump's legislative agenda. Graham, a prominent foreign policy hawk and strong supporter of Ukraine in its conflict with Russia, died unexpectedly on Saturday night, leaving his Senate seat vacant during his campaign for a fifth term. South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster is now tasked with appointing a replacement to serve out the remainder of Graham's term, with a special primary election scheduled for August to determine the next senator. This development temporarily weakens an already fragile Republican majority in the Senate, raising questions about the party's ability to advance its legislative priorities. Graham's death also has implications for U.S. foreign policy, particularly regarding relations with Russia and support for Ukraine. His recent meetings with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky underscored his commi

item.title: Microsoft's Datacenter Expansion Leads to 25% Rise in Emissions Despite Renewable Energy Goals
item.description: Microsoft's 2026 Environmental Sustainability Report reveals a significant increase in greenhouse gas emissions, rising by 25% from 13 million tons of CO2 equivalent in 2020 to 20 million tons in 2025. The surge is attributed to datacenter construction and the decision to halt purchases of renewable energy certificates. While the company matched its electricity consumption with renewables, emissions climbed despite carbon reduction initiatives. Without these measures, emissions would have reached 34 million tons. Microsoft also claims to have replenished more water than it withdrew in 2025, returning 14,278 million liters, and reused 92% of decommissioned servers through its Circular Centers program. The report highlights the challenges of balancing technological growth with environmental sustainability goals, as the company aims to achieve carbon-negative status by 2030.

item.title: White House Insiders Identify Potential Replacement for Lindsey Graham as Trump's Key Liaison
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died suddenly on Saturday night from a 'brief and sudden illness,' according to his office, marking a significant loss for the Trump administration. White House insiders have indicated that Graham's absence will immediately impact the administration's agenda, as he played a crucial role in smoothing Trump's relationships with both GOP leadership and Democrats. Graham was also a key architect of the Trump administration's domestic and foreign policy strategies. A White House official described his role as 'colossal,' emphasizing the devastation caused by his death. Insiders are now speculating about his potential successor, with some suggesting Sen. Katie Britt (R-AL) could step into the role of Trump's primary liaison. Graham's ability to communicate with Trump 'more fluently than anyone in the Senate' made him an indispensable figure. His death has left a void in the Senate's relationship with the executive branch, with officials expressing concern over the

item.title: UN Warns of Catastrophic Consequences from US-Iran Strikes
item.description: The United Nations secretary-general, António Guterres, has urged restraint from all parties involved in escalating tensions between the United States and Iran, warning of imminent 'catastrophic consequences' for the region and global economy. This comes after the U.S. launched a new round of strikes against Iran in response to an attack on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran claimed violated the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement. Iran retaliated by targeting U.S. allies in the region, prompting further U.S. strikes. Guterres emphasized the need for immediate de-escalation, stating that full-scale hostilities would have severe repercussions. The U.S. justification for its strikes was to degrade Iran's ability to attack commercial shipping vessels, while Iran accused the U.S. of interfering in regional affairs, citing the closure of the Strait of Hormuz until further notice. The situation highlights the fragile balance of power in the Middle East and the potential for wides

item.title: 18th-Century Shipwreck Uncovered with Well-Preserved 18th-Century Cargo
item.description: A remarkable 18th-century shipwreck was discovered in the Skagerrak Strait off Norway's southeastern coast, containing an exceptionally well-preserved cargo of undamaged Chinese porcelain, chandeliers, goblets, and other luxury goods. The wreck, found by Espen Saastad using a remotely operated vehicle, lies 1,970 feet beneath the surface. Archaeologists from the Norwegian Directorate for Cultural Heritage, led by Hanna Geiran, noted that the cargo is 'unlike anything ever previously found in shipwrecks in Northern Europe.' While some parts of the wreck were damaged by modern trawlers, the majority of the artifacts remain intact, offering valuable insights into 18th-century maritime trade. The ship's origins and destination are still under investigation, but clues like a brick stamped with 'Lübecker Ratsziegelei' suggest possible connections to Lübeck, Germany. The wreck is now considered a protected cultural monument, with plans to exhibit it at the Norwegian Maritime Museum in Oslo. R

item.title: Lindsey Graham Dies After Ignoring Medical Advice, Report Indicates
item.description: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died of a sudden illness, according to his office, which cited a 'brief and sudden illness' as the cause. A new Axios report suggests Graham dismissed medical advice moments before his death, with an anonymous source stating he joked about not dying now despite feeling unwell. The senator had been advised to seek immediate care but delayed treatment until after a scheduled appearance on NBC's Meet the Press. President Donald Trump claimed to have spoken with Graham Saturday night, noting the senator 'sounded a little tired' yet 'great.' Their conversation reportedly focused on Trump's controversial SAVE Act, which Graham's death is considered a setback for. Axios disputes Trump's claim as the last person to speak with Graham, citing that he had already discussed Iran-related matters before the senator allegedly dismissed medical concerns. The report highlights conflicting accounts about Graham's final hours, raising questions about his health decision

item.title: Conor McGregor Sustains Leg Injury in UFC Return, Ends Fight After 1 Minute
item.description: Conor McGregor's highly anticipated return to the UFC ended in disaster after sustaining a severe leg injury just 1 minute into his main event bout against Max Holloway at UFC 329. The former two-division champion, 37, suffered the same leg injury that plagued him in his 2021 trilogy fight against Dustin Poirier, leading to a TKO victory for Holloway. The injury occurred as McGregor attempted a kick, causing him to exit the Octagon immediately without post-fight comments. UFC CEO Dana White confirmed no pre-existing injury was evident, dismissing claims of McGregor's pre-fight limp. Despite the setback, the event generated $25 million in gate revenue, surpassing the previous record. Other notable fights included Paddy Pimblett's submission win over Benoit Saint-Denis and King Green's last-second knockout of Terrance McKinney. The article highlights McGregor's injury, the fight's abrupt conclusion, the financial success of the event, and key moments from other bouts, underscoring the dr

item.title: Understanding the Limitations and Cost-Benefit of Crypto Escrow Services
item.description: This article examines the practical limitations of crypto escrow services, emphasizing that while they provide security for payments, they do not guarantee quality or the integrity of the counterparty. Escrow mechanisms secure funds according to agreed rules but cannot ensure the seller's honesty or the product's value. Once funds are released on-chain, they are final and irreversible, unlike traditional payment methods with chargeback options. Disputes require time and evidence, with arbitrators basing decisions on submitted documentation rather than physical inspections. The cost of escrow includes platform fees, network gas, and potential arbitration charges, making it less viable for small transactions. The article highlights common pitfalls like releasing funds prematurely or vague contract terms, which can lead to avoidable losses. It concludes that escrow is most beneficial for high-value deals with strangers or when legal recourse is limited, advocating for transparent, non-cus

item.title: NRC Proposes NEPA Rule Changes to Accelerate Nuclear Licensing
item.description: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has proposed significant revisions to its National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) rules, aiming to modernize its environmental review framework for nuclear licensing. The proposed rule, published on July 7, seeks to revise 10 CFR Part 51 to focus NEPA reviews on impacts the agency can regulate, rather than broader project effects outside its authority. This shift would exclude nonradiological impacts like construction noise, air quality, and ecological effects from core NRC reviews unless the agency has authority to mitigate them. The changes target applicants seeking NRC approvals, including new reactor developers, utilities pursuing construction permits, and license-renewal applicants. The proposal aligns with recent executive orders and statutory amendments to streamline environmental reviews, potentially reducing delays in nuclear projects. Critics argue the changes could downplay environmental concerns, while proponents claim they will expe

item.title: ONR Extends Compliance Deadlines for Asbestos Management at Torness Nuclear Plant
item.description: The Office for Nuclear Regulation (ONR) has extended compliance deadlines for two improvement notices related to asbestos management at the Torness Nuclear Power Station in Scotland. The incident occurred during a planned outage in March 2026 when workers were at risk of asbestos exposure while performing overhaul activities on a steam system valve. Although no nuclear safety, public, or environmental risks were identified, the ONR acknowledged progress by both EDF and Trillium Flow Services UK Ltd. EDF now has until January 27, 2027, to address asbestos presence documentation and until October 30, 2026, to mitigate employee exposure during valve maintenance. Trillium Flow Services must comply with both notices by September 30, 2026. The ONR will continue monitoring compliance to ensure full adherence to safety protocols. This decision reflects ongoing regulatory efforts to address workplace hazards in nuclear facilities while balancing operational timelines.

item.title: Common Causes of Nighttime Urination and How to Manage Them
item.description: Nighttime urination, or nocturia, is a common issue affecting many people's quality of life. Experts suggest that factors such as pregnancy, excessive water intake before bed, and age-related conditions often contribute to frequent nighttime trips to the bathroom. Simple lifestyle adjustments, like reducing fluid consumption in the evening or avoiding caffeine and alcohol, can significantly alleviate symptoms. Additionally, underlying health conditions such as diabetes, urinary tract infections, or prostate issues may require medical attention. The article emphasizes that while occasional nighttime urination is normal, persistent disruptions warrant consultation with a healthcare professional. Lifestyle changes, including maintaining a regular sleep schedule and managing stress, can also improve bladder control. For older adults, addressing nocturia is crucial for overall well-being and sleep quality. The piece highlights the importance of recognizing when nighttime urination becomes a

item.title: CISA Had No Prepared Response Plan for Cybersecurity Incident, Report Reveals
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) admitted it lacked a pre-existing response plan for handling a cybersecurity incident in May, according to a postmortem report released on July 10, 2026. The agency revealed that its staff had to create an incident playbook during the early stages of the crisis after a contractor's employee exposed sensitive credentials and keys for accessing U.S. government systems. Independent journalist Brian Krebs reported that a security researcher with GitGuardian discovered the breach on GitHub, which an unnamed CISA contractor employee uploaded. Krebs attempted to notify the contractor but received no response, prompting CISA to take action after being contacted directly. The agency revoked and replaced all exposed credentials, stating no mission-critical data was compromised. CISA acknowledged its channels for receiving security alerts from researchers were unclear and has since implemented changes to streamline communication. Th

item.title: FBI Clarifies Role in Investigating Lindsey Graham's Death After Director's Controversial Comments
item.description: The FBI faced scrutiny after its director, Kash Patel, made a controversial social media post about the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham, sparking accusations of recklessness. Critics, including political strategists and legal experts, condemned Patel's comments for potentially fueling conspiracy theories about Graham's death, which was initially reported as a cardiac arrest. Patel's tweet, which stated the FBI was assisting local authorities, drew backlash from figures like Mike Nellis and Aaron Parnas, who argued it exacerbated speculation about a Russian plot. Despite the backlash, the FBI later clarified through a Fox News correspondent that there was no indication of foul play, with local police leading the investigation. The incident highlights tensions between political figures and law enforcement, as well as the impact of social media on public perception of sensitive issues. The article underscores the challenges of balancing transparency with the risk of misinformation

item.title: Pokemon Company Considers Price Hike and Encouraging Dual Version Purchases
item.description: An internal document from The Pokemon Company reveals past considerations to increase the price of Pokemon games beyond the original $60 per version during the Nintendo Switch era. The document also highlights strategies to boost sales by encouraging simultaneous purchases of both versions. While higher prices were discussed, they were not standardized in North America at the time. The document includes sales data showing that 41% of players bought both versions of Sun/Moon, 50% for Ultra Sun/Ultra Moon, and 23% for Let’s Go, Pikachu/Eevee. The latest insights come from the Game Freak Teraleak, which has previously uncovered leaks like the Winds and Waves game. The document outlines three pricing scenarios, with the first option maintaining current prices to maximize sales and avoid royalty increases. A second option involves a price hike with discounted double packs to encourage bulk purchases, while the third scenario risks higher overseas prices and potential sales drops. The compan

item.title: Blog Post on Nuclear Issues, Politics, and Webinars in the USA
item.description: This blog post from July 12, 2026, by Christina Macpherson highlights nuclear-related topics within the context of U.S. politics and environmental concerns. The author discusses a webinar titled 'Atomic Ties' scheduled for July 16–19 BST, which focuses on nuclear disarmament and radiation-free environments. The post also references the VFP Golden Rule Project's 22,000-mile voyage advocating for nuclear disarmament and links to in-depth nuclear-related stories on Nuclear Information and Resource Service. Categories include politics, USA, and nuclear issues, with emphasis on events like the webinar and resources such as the Fukushima Fact Sheet. The blog underscores ongoing debates about nuclear energy, safety, and its societal impacts, while critiquing corporate interests and advocating for a nuclear-free future. It also mentions global efforts to address radiation risks and highlights the role of grassroots movements in shaping public opinion on nuclear policies.

item.title: Jim Acosta Analyzes Poll Showing Dr. Annie Andrews Closing In On Lindsey Graham Amid His Death
item.description: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died Saturday night from a sudden and unexpected illness, with his office citing a 'brief and sudden illness' as the cause. Hours before his medical incident, a viral social media post circulated referencing Dr. Annie Andrews, his Democratic Senate opponent, showing she had closed in on him in recent polling. Jim Acosta, former CNN anchor, called this timing an 'eerie coincidence' and described it as 'absolutely jaw-dropping.' The poll, conducted by Impact Research between June 17–22, 2026, showed Andrews at 48% compared to Graham's 45%, within the margin of error. This marked a significant shift from earlier polls where Andrews was trailing at 36% in December. Positive candidate intros further boosted Andrews' lead, with her gaining a +6 point advantage over Graham and capturing 13% of crossover Republican votes. The article highlights how this polling data coincided with Graham's death, raising questions about the timing and implications for South C

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu Face Criticism for Leveraging Lindsey Graham's Death to Advance Political Agendas
item.description: The article details the backlash against U.S. President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for capitalizing on the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) to push their political agendas. Hours after Graham's passing, Trump appeared on NBC News to praise Graham's support for his controversial SAVE Act, a voter ID bill stalled in the Senate. Netanyahu, meanwhile, referenced their final conversation, emphasizing Graham's alleged focus on increasing U.S. military aid to Israel. Conservative Senator Mike Lee also called for the SAVE Act's passage, framing it as a tribute to Graham's legacy. Critics, including progressive media figures Brett Meiselas and Ron Filipkowski, condemned the politicians for exploiting Graham's death for personal gain, calling their actions 'sick' and hypocritical. The piece highlights the public outrage over the perceived insensitivity and the broader debate about the ethics of using public tragedies for political gain. The article al

item.title: Even Realities G2 Smart Glasses Focus on Productivity with Camera-Free Design
item.description: Even Realities' G2 smart glasses prioritize productivity over recording capabilities, featuring a monochrome neon-style heads-up display for text and information. The device lacks cameras and speakers, aiming to prevent unwanted filming during meetings or conversations. With improvements in phone connectivity and a brighter 1,200-nit display compared to its predecessor, the G2 offers enhanced features like real-time translation, teleprompting, and navigation. However, the app's accuracy issues with location services limit its practicality for everyday use. The glasses' lightweight design and UV-protective lenses make them suitable for outdoor wear, though battery life claims remain untested. While the companion R1 ring adds health tracking and control functions, its $249 price point may deter buyers. Despite solid hardware, finding a clear daily use case for these smart glasses remains challenging, highlighting Even Realities' bet on productivity-focused design over traditional camera-

item.title: Understanding Tolyamory: A Common Non-Monogamous Relationship Dynamic
item.description: The article explores the concept of tolyamory, a relationship dynamic where one or both partners tolerate their partner's non-monogamous behavior without explicit agreement. Coined by Dan Savage, this term combines 'tolerate' and 'polyamory,' highlighting relationships where infidelity is accepted but not openly endorsed. Experts like Marie Thouin and Leanne Yau discuss how tolyamory differs from polyamory and other non-monogamous practices, noting its prevalence in socially monogamous couples who maintain appearances despite underlying infidelity. Factors such as cultural norms, gender expectations, socioeconomic status, and societal stigma contribute to the persistence of tolyamorous relationships. The piece emphasizes the lack of open communication in these dynamics and the emotional complexities involved, contrasting it with consensual non-monogamy models like poly under duress (PUD) and don't ask, don't tell (DADT). The article underscores how societal pressures and fear of judgme

item.title: HFI Initiative Aims to Standardize Power-On Experience for RISC-V Hardware
item.description: The Harmonic Firmware Initiative (HFI) is developing a standardized power-on firmware experience for RISC-V hardware, aiming to replicate the x86 world's POST-like initialization and BIOS setup utility. Led by developers behind the QSOE RISC-V operating system, HFI focuses on improving the initial boot process for RISC-V boards, with the SiFive HiFive Unmatched as its primary target. The project extends U-Boot to enable proper graphics card initialization, a Power On Self Test (POST)-like screen, and a BIOS-style configuration tool. While the initiative highlights the importance of community adoption for broader impact, its success depends on uptake from major RISC-V hardware vendors. This effort represents a significant step toward enhancing RISC-V's user experience, though challenges remain in achieving widespread industry support.

item.title: Former U.S. Attorney Joyce Vance Says Senate Scrutiny of Jeffrey Epstein Files Could Shape Todd Blanche's Confirmation
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is expected to face intense scrutiny from the Senate Judiciary Committee as lawmakers consider whether to confirm him permanently as the nation's attorney general. During an appearance on MSNBC's 'MS NOW,' former U.S. Attorney Joyce Vance argued that Blanche will have to answer difficult questions about his handling of politically sensitive Justice Department matters, particularly the release and management of information related to the Jeffrey Epstein investigation. Vance contended that Blanche failed to uphold commitments he made during his earlier confirmation process, when he promised to keep political considerations out of Justice Department decisions. As an example, she criticized his reported decision to involve FBI Director Kash Patel in an investigation concerning New York Times reporting about President Donald Trump's reported $4 million Qatari jet, followed by subpoenas issued to journalists connected to that reporting. Vance described th

item.title: Stirling Proposed as Nuclear Site Amid Political Opposition
item.description: A report by Great British Energy Nuclear has suggested Stirling as a potential site for a new nuclear power plant, sparking immediate criticism from Scottish politicians. The proposal, part of a broader strategy to expand renewable energy infrastructure, highlights the site's geographical advantages and energy potential. However, local and national leaders have expressed strong opposition, arguing that the project conflicts with Scotland's commitment to renewable energy and environmental sustainability. Critics emphasize the high costs, long construction timelines, and the need to prioritize existing renewable projects over nuclear expansion. The report's recommendations include detailed feasibility studies and stakeholder consultations, but the political backlash underscores the contentious nature of nuclear energy in the region. Environmental groups and renewable energy advocates have called for a focus on wind and solar power instead, citing their lower environmental impact and fast

item.title: Trump Considers Replacement for Lindsey Graham Following His Unexpected Death
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump expressed his thoughts on replacing Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) shortly after the senator's sudden and unexpected death from a brief illness, which officials later attributed to cardiac arrest. During an interview with NBC News' Kristen Welker, Trump revealed he already had a candidate in mind for the interim replacement, though he declined to specify the individual's identity. The president emphasized that Graham's passing was unforeseen, stating, 'I said Lindsey was gonna be living forever!' Trump also praised South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster for his early endorsement of his presidential campaign, highlighting McMaster's support as a key factor in his political strategy. The article details the immediate aftermath of Graham's death, including the announcement by his office and the potential for a special election to fill the Senate vacancy. While Trump remained noncommittal about the replacement candidate, he underscored the significance of maintai

item.title: 15 Korean Skincare Products That Have Gone Viral on TikTok
item.description: This article highlights 15 Korean skincare products that have gained significant popularity on TikTok due to their effectiveness in improving skin texture, reducing acne, and providing hydration. Products like the Cosrx snail mucin essence, pore-clearing cleansing oil, and Skin1004 8-in-1 treatment masks are praised for their ability to deliver visible results with consistent use. Reviewers note that these products cater to various skin types, including acne-prone skin, and offer benefits such as reduced fine lines, tightened pores, and a brighter complexion. The article also mentions other trending items like the Beauty of Joseon glow serum, Torriden hyaluronic acid serum, and the Medicube Age-R Booster Pro device, which combine exfoliation, hydration, and advanced skincare technology. Many users emphasize the transformative effects of these products, with some reporting noticeable improvements in their skin's appearance and texture after regular use. item.title: Chiltern Vital Group and Cambridge Atomworks to Build Odin Microreactor Prototype at Berkeley Site
